skill.name: database-schema-designer
skill.description: Design SQL and NoSQL database schemas with normalization guidance, indexes, constraints, migrations, and review checklists.
skill.summary: Create production-ready database schemas and migration plans from application data requirements.
skill.author: softaworks

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found documentation, Markdown backticks, and SQL examples rather than executable code. No prompt injection, network access, credential access, data exfiltration, or malicious intent was found in the reviewed skill files. The skill is safe to publish as a documentation-driven database design assistant.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Classified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are Markdown trigger phrases and SQL examples, not Ruby backtick execution or executable shell code. They document how users can ask for schema design and do not run commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown table cells with inline code formatting. There is no interpreter call, shell command, or user-controlled execution path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Database Terms Classified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings occur in database design prose, SQL migration comments, and schema checklist headings. No hashing algorithm, encryption routine, or cryptographic API is used.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed context contains titles, descriptions, and migration metadata. No evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or comparable weak crypto usage was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: SQL Examples Classified as System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings are SQL schema examples, query analysis guidance, and migration validation snippets. They do not inspect the host system or collect environment information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are static SQL examples or query-performance examples. They do not call operating-system reconnaissance tools or access sensitive local files.

content.value_statement: Database design mistakes can cause slow queries, data corruption, and difficult migrations. This skill turns requirements into normalized schemas, indexes, constraints, migration plans, and review checklists.
actual_capabilities: Designs SQL schemas with tables, primary keys, foreign keys, constraints, and indexes.
Applies normalization guidance through 1NF, 2NF, and 3NF examples.
Recommends NoSQL embedding, referencing, and index patterns for MongoDB-style documents.
Creates reversible migration plans with up and down steps.
Reviews existing schemas against integrity, indexing, migration, and performance checklists.
Explains data type choices for money, identifiers, timestamps, strings, and relationships.
limitations: It does not connect to live databases or inspect production query plans by itself.
Generated schemas must be adapted to the exact SQL dialect and application framework.
Capacity planning still needs real workload, latency, and retention requirements.
It does not replace DBA review for high-risk production migrations.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Application Schema
use_cases.description: Turn entities, relationships, and scale hints into a first SQL schema with constraints and indexes.

use_cases.title: Review a Data Model Before Launch
use_cases.description: Check a draft schema for missing keys, weak data types, unsafe migrations, and indexing gaps.

use_cases.title: Compare SQL and NoSQL Designs
use_cases.description: Evaluate whether a workload fits normalized tables, document embedding, references, or selective denormalization.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Schema
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new project.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a database schema for a small booking app with users, listings, reservations, payments, and reviews. Include relationships and key constraints.

prompt_templates.title: Add Indexes for Access Patterns
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this schema and propose indexes for these queries: active users by email, orders by customer and date, and products by category and price.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Safe Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a reversible migration plan to split full_name into first_name and last_name without downtime. Include backfill and rollback steps.

prompt_templates.title: Design for Scale and Tradeoffs
prompt_templates.prompt: Design SQL and MongoDB options for a multi-tenant analytics product with high write volume, dashboards, retention rules, and audit logs. Compare tradeoffs.

output_examples.input: Design a schema for an online store with users, products, carts, orders, and payments.
output: A normalized entity model with user, product, cart, order, order item, and payment tables.
Primary keys, foreign keys, uniqueness rules, and delete behavior for each relationship.
Recommended indexes for customer order history, product lookup, and payment reconciliation.

output_examples.input: Review my customer table and suggest normalization improvements.
output: A list of columns that should move to separate tables or reference tables.
Data type corrections for identifiers, money, timestamps, and status fields.
A migration sequence that keeps old reads working while new columns are backfilled.

output_examples.input: Choose SQL or NoSQL for a product catalog with search, categories, and inventory updates.
output: A workload-based recommendation with read, write, and consistency tradeoffs.
Document embedding guidance for catalog attributes and references for volatile inventory.
Index suggestions for category filters, text search, and availability queries.

best_practices: Provide entities, relationships, expected query patterns, database preference, and scale estimates in the prompt.
Ask for constraints, indexes, and migration steps together so design decisions stay consistent.
Review generated schemas against your database dialect, ORM conventions, and production migration process.
anti_patterns: Do not request a schema from UI screens alone without describing domain relationships.
Do not deploy generated migration SQL without staging tests and rollback validation.
Do not accept every suggested index without checking write volume and real query frequency.

faq.question: Can this skill generate complete SQL tables?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft tables, keys, constraints, indexes, and migration guidance from your requirements.

faq.question: Does it support NoSQL design?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes MongoDB-style guidance for embedding, referencing, document shape, and indexes.

faq.question: Can it inspect my live database?
faq.answer: No. It works from the schema, queries, and requirements you provide in the conversation.

faq.question: Will the output match every SQL dialect?
faq.answer: Not automatically. Ask for PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, or another dialect when you need exact syntax.

faq.question: Can it help with migrations?
faq.answer: Yes. It can outline reversible up and down migrations and safer zero-downtime sequences.

faq.question: Is this a replacement for database administration review?
faq.answer: No. Production schemas and high-risk migrations still need tests, monitoring, and expert review.
